concepts: range-to-trend state transition with volatility expansion

thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. The only currently valid exploit lane remains ETH channel shortbias on ETH/1h, but soft 70/30 balance still favors exploration and recent attention is already too ETH-centric. Stay inside the supported universe and rotate to UMA 1h with 3 materially different specs that are not ETH failed-continuation clones and not another low-trade 4h continuation batch. Build one compression-breakout branch that only arms when funding stays negative and directional strength expands out of a tight box, one failed-breakdown value re-acceptance branch that requires fast reclaim back through value after downside excess, and one range-to-trend transition branch that only enters after volatility expansion confirms a fresh state change. The point is to test whether UMA 1h can deliver cleaner trade density and regime clarity than the overworked ETH lane and the recent zero-trade 4h continuation attempts.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 15, and max_drawdown_pct <= 9.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.00, or max_drawdown_pct > 12.0.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: soft 70/30 balance still leans explore, ETH-family exploitation remains the only valid refine lane and is already overrepresented, and rotating to supported UMA/1h avoids both ETH concentration and the recent failing 4h continuation batch pattern.
